text: onclose → cleanup → process.exit during reconnect is fatal. close() triggers onclose synchronously, killing the process before new transport is ready.
Error detection (400/not-initialized, 404/session-not-found) is partly correct but incomplete and brittle. Message substring matching is SDK-version-fragile. GET/DELETE 400 from server not covered.
MCP handshake is lifecycle-complete but hardcoded/minimal init params may not produce session-equivalent behavior if server ever depends on client capabilities.
2-hour idle TTL is not data-justified. Should be configurable via env var rather than hardcoded.
onmessage interception cleanup is one-way: timeout/failure paths can leave wrapper handler installed.
Missing liveness regression test proving reconnect doesn't trigger process.exit.
Network failure behavior (non-StreamableHTTPError) not explicitly defined.
reconnecting guard is global, not transport-identity scoped. Replacement transport close during reconnect window would be suppressed.
Detaching oldClient.onmessage before close introduces potential inbound message loss during cutover.
Handler restoration underspecified: per-promise restore on timeout conflicts with 'always restore in finally'. Need one canonical ownership rule.
Error classification position internally unresolved in Claude's response — proposed broad match then retracted it.
